diagrams.title: Verified member places an order
diagrams.purpose: Read the complete business stream from user intent through eligibility, state changes, failures, and compensation.

frames.label: Primary order stream
frames.purpose: Successful path with explicit decisions and state transitions.

frames.label: Rejected, failed, and compensated outcomes
frames.purpose: Terminal branches; no ambiguous dead ends.

nodes.label: Place order
nodes.summary: Verified member submits product, quantity, payment token.

nodes.label: Member eligible?
nodes.summary: Role MEMBER, status VERIFIED, not suspended.

nodes.label: Order: NONE → PENDING
nodes.summary: Create immutable product and price snapshot.

nodes.label: order.placed
nodes.summary: Publish asynchronously for confirmation and fulfillment.

nodes.label: Rejected: product
nodes.summary: Inactive product or closed sale window.

nodes.label: Order → FAILED
nodes.summary: Reservation failed; payment is never called.

nodes.label: Decline: release stock
nodes.summary: Payment declined or timed out; fail order and release reservation.

edges.label: published
edges.condition: response does not wait for worker

legend.label: ↻ State
legend.meaning: Shows entity state before and after.

evidence.excerpt: Visual model mirrors the structured stream.

answers.who_starts: See the actor or trigger shown in the diagram.
answers.what_they_want: Read the complete business stream from user intent through eligibility, state changes, failures, and compensation.
answers.key_decisions: All modeled decisions and branch conditions are shown.
answers.success_change: Terminal outcomes and state changes are shown in the diagram.

does_not_answer: This Atlas diagram does not replace source, test, or runtime verification.
stop_reasons: Inactive product or closed sale window.
Reservation failed; payment is never called.

diagrams.title: Product orderability and order state lifecycle
diagrams.purpose: Make product-state eligibility and order-state transitions visible together without pretending they are one state machine.

frames.label: Product lifecycle
frames.purpose: State controls whether orderability can even be considered.

frames.label: Order lifecycle
frames.purpose: Order state transitions and terminal outcomes.

frames.label: Cross-entity orderability gate
frames.purpose: Rules that combine member, product, time, and inventory.

nodes.label: ACTIVE
nodes.summary: Orderable only while sale window is open.

nodes.label: PAUSED
nodes.summary: Visible policy may vary; ordering rejected.

nodes.label: Orderability gate
nodes.summary: member VERIFIED ∧ product ACTIVE ∧ sale window open ∧ stock sufficient.

legend.label: ↻ State
legend.meaning: Shows entity state before and after.
